{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyogo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=draft}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[Mefujinja Station]] on the [[Hankyu]] [[Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]
Nakayamasōen Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakayamasoen Kofun]][[en:Nakayamasōen Kofun]][[ja:中山荘園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11364274]] Wikidata name: Nakayamasoen Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.82|N|135.363056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakayamasoen Kofun]][[en:Nakayamasōen Kofun]][[ja:中山荘園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364274]] Wikidata name: Nakayamasoen Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.82|N|135.363056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]
Nakayamasōen Kofun

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakayamasoen Kofun]][[en:Nakayamasōen Kofun]][[ja:中山荘園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364274]] Wikidata name: Nakayamasoen Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.82|N|135.363056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakayamasoen Kofun]][[en:Nakayamasōen Kofun]][[ja:中山荘園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11364274]] Wikidata name: Nakayamasoen Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.82|N|135.363056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka, Hyōgo]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the [[:en:Hankyu|Hankyu]] [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)]]

The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the {{ill|Hankyu|en|Hankyu|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu}} [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}} [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|WD=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the {{ill|Hankyu|en|Hankyu|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu}} [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Nakayamasoen Kofun}} *[https://www.city.takarazuka.hyogo.jp/kyoiku/rekishi/1026244/1003723.html Takarazuka City home page]

The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the {{ill|Hankyu|en|Hankyu|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Nakayamasōen Kofun | native_name =中山荘園古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Nakayamasoen Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_type = Japan Hyōgo Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakayamasōen Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.82|N|135.363056|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = mid-7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|octagonal kofun|WD=Q11391751|1=Octagonal Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|49|12.38|N|135|21|47.43|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nakayamasōen Kofun'''|中山荘園古墳|}} is an {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Nakayamasōen neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|WD=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka, Hyōgo|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1999.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/140237|title=中山荘園古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nakayamasōen Kofun is a very rare {{nihongo|''{{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=hakkaku-fun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun}}, which is octagonal. It is located at the tip of a ridge extending southward from the western part of the hills of Mt. Nagao in eastern Hyōgo Prefecture. It was discovered in 1982 and an [[archaeological excavation]] was conducted from 1983 to 1984. The tumulus has a diagonal length of about 13 meters and a height of about 2.6 meters. It is surrounded by two rows of octagonal outer guard stones on each side, and a terrace-like facility that is thought to be an altar on the south side. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a small horizontal stone chamber orientated to the south. No {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref> From the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, keyhole-shaped Kofuns (''zenpō-kōen-fun'') were no longer built, and the number of Kofuns for chieftains decreased considerably, but most of them, including the imperial mausoleum, were square-type {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}. Of the over 160,000 ''kofun'' in Japan, only 17 are octagonal Kofuns, accounting for less than one percent, and most of these were constructed in the middle of the 7th century, and were the tombs for emperors. Although the person buried in this tumulus is unknown, the location is in close proximity to the [[Mefu Shrine|Mefu Jinja]], which was serving the Wakayuza-branch of the [[Mononobe clan]] during this period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 10-minute walk from [[:en:Mefujinja Station|Mefujinja Station]] on the {{ill|Hankyu|en|Hankyu|WD=Q473058|lt=Hankyu|1=Hankyu}} [[:en:Takarazuka Main Line|Takarazuka Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Nakayamashouen Kohun(digging up).jpg|During excavation Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-1.jpg|Burial Chamber Nakayamasoen Kofun, sekishitsu-2.jpg|BurialChamber </gallery> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|WD=Q104629006|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hyōgo)}}
